payload.answer: There is a conflict: the existing memory says "Sara is my daughter," but the new input says "Sara is my niece." I cannot assume which one is correct or automatically overwrite the existing memory. Safe actions available are: approve_supersession to accept the new statement as replacing the old one, keep_both to retain both statements if they may refer to different people or both are contextually true, reject_new to keep the existing memory and discard the new input, or edit to clarify the relationship or distinguish between different people named Sara.

entity_resolution.reason: The new input describes Sara as the user's niece, while an existing memory describes Sara as the user's daughter. These family relationships may conflict if they refer to the same Sara, so the system should not automatically replace or merge them.

details: Conflict detected between existing memory and new input.
Awaiting one of the safe actions: approve_supersession, keep_both, reject_new, or edit.
